faqs.question: How do I add my first property?
faqs.answer: Navigate to Properties from the sidebar, then click 'Add Property'. Fill in the address and details. You can add compliance documents at this stage or later.

faqs.question: What's the difference between a Reference and a Tenancy?
faqs.answer: A Reference checks a potential tenant's background, employment, and credit. Once approved, you convert it into a Tenancy, which represents an active rental agreement. References are the 'before' and Tenancies are the 'after'.

faqs.question: How do I subscribe to a plan?
faqs.answer: Go to the Tenancies tab and you will see a prompt to subscribe. Choose Standard (£11.99/month, up to 10 properties) or Professional (£24.99/month, unlimited). Enter your payment details via the secure Stripe checkout.

faqs.question: Can I add an SPV or company as the landlord?
faqs.answer: Yes. Go to the Landlords tab and click 'Add Company'. Enter the company name, registration number, directors, and bank details. You can then select it as the landlord on properties and agreements.

faqs.question: How does RentGoose track my rent?
faqs.answer: When you activate a tenancy, the first month's rent is auto-added to RentGoose. Subsequent months auto-populate as they become due. You mark payments as received when rent arrives, and RentGoose tracks outstanding balances automatically.

faqs.question: What happens if my tenant doesn't pay?
faqs.answer: If auto-chase is enabled, RentGoose sends automatic reminder emails at 7, 14, 21, and 28 days overdue. You can also send manual quick chase reminders or generate formal arrears letters at any time.

faqs.question: How do I cancel my subscription?
faqs.answer: Go to Settings and find the billing section. You can cancel your subscription there. It remains active until the end of your current billing period, then your account reverts to PAYG.

faqs.question: Do I need a subscription to use references?
faqs.answer: No. References are available on all plans, including PAYG. On PAYG, each reference costs £17.50. Standard subscribers pay £13 per reference, and Professional subscribers pay £12.50 per reference.

faqs.question: What is rent up front?
faqs.answer: Rent up front charges multiple months of rent at tenancy start instead of just the first month. Common for students or tenants without UK credit history. You can request 1-60 months when creating initial monies.

faqs.question: What is pro-rata rent?
faqs.answer: Pro-rata rent covers partial months when the move-in date doesn't align with the rent due date. For example, moving in on the 15th when rent is due on the 1st. PropertyGoose calculates this automatically.

faqs.question: What compliance documents are required?
faqs.answer: At minimum: Gas Safety Certificate (annual), EPC (valid for 10 years), and EICR (every 5 years). PropertyGoose tracks expiry dates and alerts you when renewals are due.

faqs.question: How do I send an offer form to a tenant?
faqs.answer: Go to Offers, click 'Send Offer', enter the tenant's email and property details. They'll receive an email with a link to complete the offer form online.

faqs.question: How do I start a reference check?
faqs.answer: Go to Offers, find an approved offer, and click 'Start Reference'. Pay for the reference via the secure checkout, and the tenant will receive an email to complete their reference questionnaire.

faqs.question: What does 'Pending' status mean on a reference?
faqs.answer: Pending means the reference has been sent but the tenant hasn't completed their questionnaire yet. They need to fill in employment, landlord, and personal details before checks can proceed.

faqs.question: What does 'In Progress' status mean?
faqs.answer: In Progress means the tenant has submitted their information and the system is verifying details with employers, previous landlords, and credit agencies. This typically takes 24-48 hours.

faqs.question: What does 'Passed' status mean?
faqs.answer: Passed means all reference checks have been completed satisfactorily. The tenant's employment, previous tenancy, and credit checks all met the required criteria. You can now convert to a tenancy.

faqs.question: What does 'Failed' status mean?
faqs.answer: Failed indicates issues were found during checks. Review the reference report for details - it could be adverse credit, unverifiable employment, or poor landlord feedback. You may request a guarantor.

faqs.question: How do I convert a reference to a tenancy?
faqs.answer: Open the passed reference and click 'Convert to Tenancy'. Confirm the tenancy details (dates, rent) and click 'Convert'. This creates the tenancy record and moves the reference to completed. You need a Standard or Professional subscription to access Tenancies.

faqs.question: Why can't I convert this reference to a tenancy?
faqs.answer: References can only be converted when status is 'Passed'. If status is Pending, In Progress, or Failed, conversion isn't available. Check the reference report to see what's blocking progress. You also need a subscription to access the Tenancies tab.

faqs.question: How do I protect a deposit?
faqs.answer: Open the tenancy, click 'Protect Deposit'. If TDS or mydeposits integration is connected, details are sent automatically. Otherwise, record protection details manually. You must protect within 30 days of receipt.

faqs.question: What happens if I don't protect a deposit in time?
faqs.answer: You must protect deposits within 30 days and provide prescribed information. Failure to comply can result in penalties of 1-3 times the deposit amount and inability to serve Section 21 notices.

faqs.question: How do I generate a tenancy agreement?
faqs.answer: Go to Agreements > Generate. You can import data from existing references or enter details manually. Complete the step-by-step wizard covering tenants, property, terms, and your details as landlord.

faqs.question: How do I send an agreement for electronic signing?
faqs.answer: After generating an agreement, click 'Send for Signing'. Verify all recipient emails are correct and click 'Send'. Each party (tenants and you) receives an email with a secure signing link.

faqs.question: What happens when a tenant submits an offer?
faqs.answer: You receive an email notification and the offer appears in your Offers list with 'Submitted' status. Review the details and either accept it to proceed to referencing or decline it. The tenant is notified of your decision.

faqs.question: How do I change a move-in date?
faqs.answer: Open the reference or tenancy and click 'Edit'. Update the start/move-in date field and save. The dashboard calendar will automatically reflect the new date.

faqs.question: How do I add or remove a tenant from an existing tenancy?
faqs.answer: Open the tenancy and click 'Tenant Change'. Choose to add, remove, or replace a tenant. New tenants must complete references. Remember to update deposit protection with the new names.

faqs.question: What is a Section 8 notice?
faqs.answer: A Section 8 notice seeks possession due to tenancy breaches, usually rent arrears. You specify grounds (reasons) and the notice period varies by ground. Legal advice is recommended before serving.

faqs.question: How do I increase the rent?
faqs.answer: Open the tenancy and click 'Rent Increase'. Enter the new amount and effective date. PropertyGoose generates a Section 13 notice. Serve it to the tenant with proper notice (usually 1 month for monthly tenancies).

faqs.question: Why can't I increase the rent yet?
faqs.answer: Rent can only be increased once every 12 months for periodic tenancies. If you increased rent recently, you must wait until 12 months have passed before the next increase takes effect.

faqs.question: How do I connect my TDS account?
faqs.answer: Go to Settings > Integrations, find TDS, and click 'Connect'. Enter your TDS Member ID and API key from your TDS portal. Test the connection and save. You can then protect deposits directly from PropertyGoose.

faqs.question: What does the dashboard calendar show?
faqs.answer: The calendar displays upcoming move-in dates from both references and tenancies. Click on a date to see details. It helps you prepare for upcoming tenancy starts and track your schedule.

faqs.question: What are the quick stats on the dashboard?
faqs.answer: Quick stats show: Active References (in progress), Pending Tenancies (not yet started), Active Tenancies (current), and properties count. Click any stat to go directly to that section.

faqs.question: What happens when a compliance certificate expires?
faqs.answer: PropertyGoose alerts you before expiry with warnings on the property page and dashboard. Expired certificates show in red. You should arrange renewals before expiry to remain legally compliant.

faqs.question: How do I change where notifications are sent?
faqs.answer: Go to Settings and update the notification email fields. These control where system notifications go for offers and references.

faqs.question: Can I resend a reference request to the tenant?
faqs.answer: Yes. Open the reference and click 'Resend Invite'. The tenant will receive a new email with the link to complete their questionnaire. Useful if the original email was missed or expired.

faqs.question: How do I reference multiple tenants for one property?
faqs.answer: Create a reference for each tenant individually, selecting the same property. All references can be converted to a single joint tenancy once they pass. Group them by using the same property address.

faqs.question: What do the tenancy statuses mean?
faqs.answer: Pending: not yet started (future start date). Active: currently running. Ended: tenancy has finished. Each status determines what actions are available on the tenancy.

faqs.question: How do I change my password?
faqs.answer: Go to Settings > Profile. Scroll to 'Change Password'. Enter your new password (minimum 6 characters), confirm it, and click 'Update Password'. Your new password takes effect immediately.

faqs.question: When does the new Periodic Tenancy Agreement apply?
faqs.answer: From 1 May 2026 for all new English tenancies. PropertyGoose automatically selects the APTA template for any move-in date on or after this date. Welsh agreements are unaffected.

faqs.question: Can I still include a break clause in the agreement?
faqs.answer: No. Break clauses are not permitted in Assured Periodic Tenancies under the Renters Rights Act 2026. You can still add other special clauses via the Properties tab.

faqs.question: I have student tenancies on ASTs already signed — what do I need to do?
faqs.answer: You must serve the compliance pack to all affected tenants before 30 May 2026. The compliance pack information sheet covers the AST to periodic conversion. Send it via the Tenancies tab.

faqs.question: Will utility switching notify utilities for bills-included properties?
faqs.answer: No. Properties marked as Bills Included in PropertyGoose are automatically excluded from all utility switching notifications. No action is needed — the system handles this automatically.

faqs.question: How do I opt out of utility switching?
faqs.answer: Go to Settings and untick the Just Move In toggle. This will disable utility notifications for your account. You will then need to notify utilities manually.

faqs.question: Is RentGoose available on all plans?
faqs.answer: RentGoose is available on Standard and Professional subscription plans. PAYG users do not have access to RentGoose. Subscribe to Standard (£11.99/month) or Professional (£24.99/month) to unlock rent tracking.
